title: GRAPH_INTERRUPT_DIAGNOSTIC_AND_NO_INTERRUPT_MODEL_SMOKE_PLAN

pressure_assessment.reason: Read-only scan only — no code execution, no API calls, no tools, no MCP, no side effects

lane_0_pressure_assessment.rationale: All LANE 0 checks in R137 passed; worker executed; interrupt triggered without model API call; ready for diagnostic

lane_1_r137_reclassification.reclassification_rationale: Worker path confirmed: run reaches 'running' then 'interrupted' in ~9s without model API call; graph-level interrupt fires even when interrupt_before_nodes is None
lane_1_r137_reclassification.lane_8_classification: Outcome D — graph-level interrupt triggered; worker path exercised correctly
lane_1_r137_reclassification.key_evidence: run_manager.set_status(running) succeeded at 11:19:44, set_status(interrupted) succeeded at 11:19:53, no CancelledError, no error message in record.error

lane_2_interrupt_source_mapping.scan_scope: worker.py / services.py / thread_runs.py / lead_agent/agent.py / langchain.agents factory

interrupt_before_sources.condition: Only set when interrupt_before is truthy

interrupt_after_sources.condition: Only set when interrupt_after is truthy

langchain_create_agent_default_interrupt.finding: interrupt_before and interrupt_after are passed to graph.compile() — LangGraph uses these to set interrupt_before_nodes / interrupt_after_nodes on the CompiledStateGraph at compile time. The compiled graph has NO default interrupt nodes when both are None.
langchain_create_agent_default_interrupt.conclusion: create_agent() does NOT set any default interrupt nodes when interrupt_before=None and interrupt_after=None

lead_agent_agent_py_interrupt_check.grep_result: No matches for interrupt_before_nodes|interrupt_after_nodes in entire agents directory
lead_agent_agent_py_interrupt_check.conclusion: make_lead_agent does NOT set interrupt_before_nodes or interrupt_after_nodes anywhere

lane_2_interrupt_source_mapping.finding: interrupt_before and interrupt_after are NOT set by default anywhere in the chain. They must be explicitly passed via POST /runs body.

lane_3_request_payload_influence.question: Can POST /runs payload disable interrupt via explicit interrupt_before: [] / interrupt_after: []?

findings.fact: worker.py:201-204 sets interrupt nodes ONLY when truthy: if interrupt_before: agent.interrupt_before_nodes = interrupt_before
findings.implication: Passing interrupt_before=[] (empty list) is falsy in Python, so agent.interrupt_before_nodes would NOT be set

findings.fact: LangGraph CompiledStateGraph.compile() with interrupt_before=None → graph has no interrupt nodes
findings.implication: Even if empty list bypassed the worker check, graph.compile(None) would not add interrupt nodes
compile() receives None, graph has no built-in interrupts

findings.fact: interrupt_before_nodes is a writable attribute set at runtime in worker.py:202
findings.implication: Runtime assignment of interrupt nodes is independent of what create_agent() set at compile time

lane_3_request_payload_influence.conclusion: Explicit interrupt_before=[] / interrupt_after=[] in POST /runs body would disable interrupts at the worker level (empty list is falsy → assignment skipped). HOWEVER: the interrupt still needs to be triggered by something. An empty POST /runs with [] would likely result in success, not interrupted. The interrupt in R137 was NOT caused by interrupt_before/interrupt_after.

lane_4_worker_graph_execution_boundary.question: What sets status=interrupted in worker when interrupt_before_nodes=None?

code_path_analysis.code: if record.abort_event.is_set(): ... else: await run_manager.set_status(run_id, RunStatus.success)
code_path_analysis.finding: Normal astream completion → success. abort_event NOT set → success path.
Task cancellation path. BUT: no CancelledError was logged in R137; task was NOT externally cancelled.

code_path_analysis.code: except Exception as exc: → error status
code_path_analysis.finding: Exception path. No exception in R137.

abort_event_analysis.source_1: manager.py:159-160: cancel() sets abort_event and abort_action
abort_event_analysis.source_2: manager.py:201-208: create_or_reject with multitask_strategy in (interrupt, rollback) sets abort_event on inflight runs
abort_event_analysis.conclusion: abort_event is set by EXTERNAL calls to cancel() or by create_or_reject() clearing inflight runs. R137 did NOT call cancel().

interrupt_hypothesis.hypothesis: LangGraph's interrupt_before_nodes triggers a special interrupt that does NOT raise CancelledError but sets status=interrupted via abort_event mechanism
interrupt_hypothesis.investigation: The interrupt_before_nodes attribute on CompiledStateGraph causes LangGraph to pause at the specified node and issue an interrupt. When astream is called with a stream_mode, an interrupt at the first node would cause astream to return early with interrupt state.
interrupt_hypothesis.key_observation: In R137, worker.py:201-204 sets interrupt_before_nodes from interrupt_before parameter. Since interrupt_before=None from POST /runs body, worker.py:201 check fails: if interrupt_before: → False → interrupt_before_nodes NOT set on agent
interrupt_hypothesis.conclusion: interrupt_before_nodes was NOT set in R137. The interrupt must come from a different source.

langgraph_astream_interrupt_behavior.finding: graph.compile(interrupt_before=None, interrupt_after=None) creates a graph with NO interrupt nodes
langgraph_astream_interrupt_behavior.behavior: When astream is called on such a graph with input={"messages": [...]}, it should process through model node and return. No interrupt should occur.
langgraph_astream_interrupt_behavior.r137_observation: 9 seconds elapsed between 'running' and 'interrupted'. Model API was NOT called (sk-c... truncated). This is NOT enough time for a real API call + response cycle, but is consistent with a graph-level interrupt firing at the first checkpoint.

lane_4_worker_graph_execution_boundary.leading_hypothesis: The interrupt is triggered by LangGraph's checkpointer behavior when the agent is resumed from a checkpoint that has interrupt state from a prior execution. However, R137 created a NEW run (new run_id), so this shouldn't apply. ALTERNATIVE: The interrupt_before_nodes or interrupt_after_nodes is being set somewhere outside the explicit worker.py assignment.

lane_5_resume_path_feasibility.question: Is there a POST /runs/{run_id}/resume endpoint?

findings.purpose: Update thread state (e.g. for human-in-the-loop resume or title rename)
findings.mechanism: Writes a new checkpoint that merges values into the latest channel values
findings.note: This is the RESUME mechanism — writes a checkpoint with new values that the next run can resume from

lane_5_resume_path_feasibility.resume_mechanism: Resume is NOT a /runs/{run_id}/resume endpoint. Instead: (1) POST /threads/{thread_id}/state with values updates the checkpoint; (2) The next POST /runs with checkpoint_id parameter references that checkpoint. This is the LangGraph Platform checkpoint-based resume pattern.
lane_5_resume_path_feasibility.conclusion: No single-step resume endpoint exists. Resume requires: (1) updating thread state via /threads/{thread_id}/state, then (2) creating a new run with checkpoint_id parameter.

lane_6_r139_no_interrupt_one_model_smoke.objective: Verify that POST /runs with interrupt_before=[] / interrupt_after=[] produces a clean success outcome (no interrupt, model API called, run completes successfully)

constraints.interrupt_before_nodes: Must be explicitly disabled via interrupt_before=[] in request body

lane_6_r139_no_interrupt_one_model_smoke.pressure_assessment: XXL — real model API call with no interrupts and no side effects
lane_6_r139_no_interrupt_one_model_smoke.expected_outcome: Outcome A — clean success; model API called exactly once; run completes successfully
test_steps: LANE 1: Preflight — MINIMAX_API_KEY, workspace clean, health_200
LANE 2: Model injection — same as R137
LANE 4: POST /runs with interrupt_before=[] and interrupt_after=[]
LANE 5: Poll run status until completed/success (not interrupted)
LANE 6: Verify model API called exactly once
LANE 7: Verify no tool calls, no MCP, no DB/JSONL writes
LANE 8: Result classification — expect Outcome A (clean success)
key_differences_from_r137: interrupt_before=[] (empty list — falsy, disables interrupt_before_nodes assignment)
interrupt_after=[] (empty list — falsy, disables interrupt_after_nodes assignment)

findings.result: interrupt fires WITHOUT model API call
confirmed — worker.py:201 check fails when interrupt_before=None
findings.note: This means interrupt is NOT caused by explicit interrupt_before_nodes setting

findings.result: BLOCKED — need to understand interrupt source first
findings.note: R137 interrupt fired without model API call and without explicit interrupt nodes set. This means the interrupt source is NOT in the interrupt_before_nodes assignment path.

lane_7_tool_enabled_readiness_gate.conclusion: R139 (no-interrupt smoke) must run before R138_TOOL_ENABLED_SMOKE. The unexpected interrupt in R137 (fires with interrupt_before_nodes=None) must be characterized first. If R139 with interrupt_before=[] still gets interrupted, the interrupt source is in the graph itself, not the interrupt_before_nodes assignment.

new_findings.description: interrupt_before_nodes=None yet status=interrupted fires at worker.py:279 in ~9s without model API call
new_findings.evidence: R137 timeline: running@11:19:44, interrupted@11:19:53, no API call, no CancelledError, no error message
possible_sources: LangGraph checkpointer interrupt state from prior checkpoint (unlikely — new run_id)
LangGraph's own internal interrupt for empty/initial graph input
Something in the middleware chain triggering an interrupt
The abort_event being set by create_or_reject during inflight cleanup

new_findings.description: Resume is NOT a /runs/{run_id}/resume single endpoint — uses POST /threads/{thread_id}/state + checkpoint_id parameter pattern
new_findings.evidence: thread_runs.py has no resume endpoint; threads.py:462 has update_thread_state for checkpoint updates

critical_finding: The interrupt in R137 fired even though interrupt_before_nodes was NOT set (worker.py:201 check fails when interrupt_before=None). This means the interrupt source is NOT in the explicit interrupt_before_nodes assignment path. The interrupt must originate from: (1) LangGraph's internal behavior with the empty graph input, (2) middleware, or (3) the checkpointer. R139 with interrupt_before=[] must run to determine if the interrupt persists without explicit interrupt nodes.
